subject: .bodylist attribute on mechanics classes
truth: Stop using .bodylist; use .bodies instead on all SymPy Physics Mechanics classes.
quote: All methods classes in SymPy Physics Mechanics now use the same set of

subject: .forcelist attribute on mechanics classes
truth: Stop using .forcelist; use .loads instead on all SymPy Physics Mechanics classes.

subject: .rank on N-dimensional arrays
truth: Stop using .rank to get the number of dimensions of an N-dimensional array; use .ndim property or get_ndim() function instead.
quote: N-dimensional arrays have used to method `rank` to return the number of dimensions. Unfortunately this not the proper term and it can be confused with the concept of matrix rank. From now on, please use `.ndim` property or the `get_ndim(...)` function instead.

subject: tensor_product_simp from sympy.physics.quantum
truth: Stop calling tensor_product_simp (and tensor_product_simp_Mul, tensor_product_simp_Pow); they are now redundant because the transformations are applied automatically.
quote: SymPy are not {class}`~.Expr` because they represent some other type of

subject: IdentityOperator from sympy.physics.quantum
truth: Stop using IdentityOperator; use S.One as the multiplicative identity for quantum operators and states instead.
quote: multiplicative identity for quantum operators and states.

subject: aesaracode module from sympy.printing
truth: Stop using sympy's aesaracode module; it is deprecated because aesara is unmaintained and cannot be installed on Python 3.13.
quote: sympy's aesaracode module is deprecated because aesara itself is umaintained and cannot be installed on Python 3.13.

subject: Body class
truth: Stop using Body; use RigidBody for rigid bodies and Particle for particles instead.
quote: Previously, one could create a simple rigid body or particle using only the

associative: how to make a rigid body

subject: string fallback in sympify()
old: sympify() would fall back to str(expr) and parse it via parse_expr when all other conversion methods failed
truth: sympify() no longer falls back to converting unrecognized objects to strings and parsing them; it will raise an error instead.
quote: The sympify function would previously convert an unrecognized object to a string and retry sympification. This was deprecated in SymPy 1.6 and was removed in SymPy 1.13.

from_fact: sympify() would fall back to str(expr) and parse it via parse_expr when all other conversion methods failed

subject: sympy.matrices.common and sympy.matrices.matrices modules
old: These modules existed and could be imported from
truth: Importing anything from sympy.matrices.common or sympy.matrices.matrices is deprecated and these modules will be removed in a future release.
quote: More generally importing anything from the sympy.matrices.common or sympy.matrices.matrices modules in which these classes are defined is deprecated. These modules will be removed in a future release of SymPy.

from_fact: these modules existed and could be imported from

subject: sympify() default behaviour
old: sympify() uses string parsing (parse_expr) for unknown types
new: sympify(strict=True) or _sympify() should be used; strict=True will become default
truth: Stop using sympify() without strict=True in library code; use _sympify() or sympify(strict=True) instead to avoid 100x slowdowns and security issues from string eval.
quote: Using `strict=True` will at some point be the default for all library code

from_fact: sympify() uses string parsing (parse_expr) for unknown types
sympify(strict=true) or _sympify() should be used; strict=true will become default

subject: DMP.rep attribute
old: DMP.rep (direct attribute access returning a list)
truth: Stop accessing DMP.rep directly; use DMP.to_list() instead, as .rep now emits a deprecation warning and DUP_Flint has no analogous attribute.
quote: Accessing `.rep` will still generate a list but now gives a deprecation warning. Instead of `.rep` use the `DMP.to_list()` method

from_fact: dmp.rep (direct attribute access returning a list)

subject: pkgdata module
truth: Stop importing sympy.pkgdata; the module has been deprecated and removed.
quote: Deprecate the pkgdata module

subject: Eq.rewrite(Add)
truth: Stop using eq.rewrite(Add) to get x - y; write eq.lhs - eq.rhs instead.
quote: To silence SymPy deprecation warnings, add a filter using the

truth: Stop setting markers, annotations, fill, rectangles as attributes on a Plot object; pass them as keyword arguments to the plotting function instead.
quote: ### Deprecate markers, annotations, fill, rectangles of the Plot class

truth: Stop importing inertia from sympy.physics.mechanics.functions; import it from sympy.physics.mechanics.inertia instead.

subject: ordered comparisons on modular integers (GF elements)
old: a < b works on GF elements
new: raises TypeError with flint ground types, deprecated otherwise
truth: Ordered comparisons like a < b on GF modular integer elements now raise TypeError when ground types are flint, and are deprecated (with warning) otherwise.
quote: This will now fail with TypeError when the ground types are set to flint. When the ground types are not flint these comparisons are now deprecated

from_fact: a < b works on gf elements
raises typeerror with flint ground types, deprecated otherwise

subject: ModularInteger.to_int() method
truth: Stop calling a.to_int() on modular integer elements; use K.to_int(a) or int(a) instead.
quote: The elements of a modular integer domain have a to_int() method that is deprecated since SymPy 1.13

subject: int(a) on modular integer elements
new: int(a) always returns nonnegative integer, ignoring symmetric flag
truth: int(a) on a GF element now always returns a nonnegative integer, whereas before SymPy 1.13 it was equivalent to a.to_int() which could return negative values when symmetric=True.
quote: the behaviour of int(a) was changed in SymPy 1.13: in previous versions it was equivalent to a.to_int()

from_fact: int(a) always returns nonnegative integer, ignoring symmetric flag

subject: sympy.partitions_.npartitions
new: from sympy import npartitions (or from sympy.functions import npartitions)

old: generalized coordinates/speeds stored as list with unique auto-naming per joint type
new: generalized coordinates/speeds stored as Matrix with auto-naming format q_<joint.name> and u_<joint.name>

generalized coordinates/speeds stored as matrix with auto-naming format q_<joint.name> and u_<joint.name>
